I've never seen a research paper or presentation utilize any form of a production function that is not CES.  why is imposing CES so important in our models? **Correct Answering Passage:**  The dominance of CES (Constant Elasticity of Substitution) production functions in economic modeling is primarily due to their flexibility and analytical tractability. CES functions allow for a constant, tunable degree of substitutability between inputs—such as capital and labor—via a single elasticity parameter, which can capture a wide spectrum ranging from perfect substitutes (linear case) to perfect complements (Leontief case), with the Cobb-Douglas as an intermediate case. This generality means that by choosing an appropriate elasticity value, a wide set of real-world production relationships can be approximated within the same functional form. Fitness: All value-creating economic transformations and transactions produce artifacts and/or actions that are fit for human purposes.
